parent_plan: PRE-HARDWARE-PREP-PLAN-001 — Workstream C (feature and entity completeness)
description: Per-board / per-module feature and entity matrix. This is the NEW source of truth for which Home Assistant sensor entities and product HA features each Sense360 board/module YAML is supposed to expose, and the present-vs-required audit against the YAML that exists today. It complements config/kit-intent-matrix.json (which is MODULE COMPOSITION only and says nothing about per-product entities/features). This file is PLANNING / AUDIT only: it does not add, remove, or change any entity or feature in any board, feature, expansion, hardware, or product YAML, and it does not imply WebFlash exposure, stable promotion, firmware builds, or release artifacts. The actual addition of any missing entity or feature happens in the per-board implementation slices named in `queued_fill_slice`, never in the PR that introduces this matrix. See docs/feature-entity-matrix.md.

row_status_legend.present: An entity / feature that satisfies the required row exists in the current YAML for this board/module (canonical base or, where noted, a named overlay variant).
row_status_legend.partial: Some but not all of the required row is satisfied: e.g. the entity exists only in an overlay variant and not the canonical base, the driver part differs from the schematic BOM, the entity is declared but unvalidated/compile-pending, or only the package-layer output exists with no user-facing entity.
row_status_legend.missing: No entity / feature satisfying the required row exists in any current YAML for this board/module.

derivation_legend.design-derived: Row is derived from the board BOM / schematic / pin-map. It can be filled now from the schematic and fully pinned once the BOM/gerbers land. It is NOT product intent — a populated sensor is expected to surface an entity.
derivation_legend.product-intent: Row is product intent (modes, presets, dedicated night-light, fan speed/preset UX, exposed comfort indices, etc.). It is NOT derivable from the schematic and MUST be marked needs-operator-confirm wherever the intended behaviour is not already obvious from existing YAML or product docs. The matrix never invents a feature set.

confirm_flags.none: No operator confirmation required to fill: the requirement is unambiguous from the schematic BOM (design-derived) or already evidenced in existing YAML/product docs.
confirm_flags.needs-operator-confirm: Product intent is not established. The operator must specify whether this feature/entity is intended before any fill slice may proceed. Do not fabricate it.
confirm_flags.needs-schematic-reconcile: A design-derived row whose final part/driver/pin is not yet locked (schematic cataloged_unverified, BOM/gerbers not landed, or the live YAML driver differs from the catalog BOM). Fill is blocked on the schematic/BOM, not on the operator.

boards.notes: ESP32-S3-WROOM-1 hub. Owns the on-board relay K1 (GPIO3) and the shared core_i2c / RoomIQ UART buses. Device-health and diagnostics entities (uptime, WiFi, restart/safe-mode, internal temperature, CPU duty) are defined in the hardware core config and the device_health.yaml / diagnostics.yaml feature packages, not duplicated per board.

rows.present_as: switch main_relay
rows.notes: Core owns the GPIO output; the FanRelay expansion proxies it via a template switch to avoid double-binding GPIO3.

rows.row: Device health entities (uptime, WiFi signal, restart, safe mode, internal temperature)
rows.present_as: device_health.yaml + hardware core config
rows.notes: Intentionally centralised in the hardware core config to avoid duplicate-id breakage across profiles.

boards.notes: Merged comfort + presence room-sensor board. Catalog BOM (config/hardware-catalog.json): PIR (EKMC1601111), LD2450, SEN0609/C4001, LTR-303ALS light, SHT4x temp/humidity, BMP581 pressure. The current canonical YAML composes a VEML7700 ALS + SHT4x climate half and an LD2450 radar half only — three BOM sensors (SEN0609 second radar, EKMC PIR, BMP581 pressure) surface NO entity today, and the ambient-light part in YAML (VEML7700) differs from the catalog BOM part (LTR-303ALS-01).

rows.present_as: sensor s360_temperature ("Temperature", calibrated canonical output; raw comfort_ceiling_temperature stays internal, legacy comfort_temperature_display preserved disabled-by-default)
rows.notes: ROOMIQ-FRAMEWORK-001: customer Temperature Offset calibration applied once inside include/sense360/roomiq_engine.h; stale data goes unknown, never frozen.

rows.present_as: sensor s360_humidity ("Humidity", calibrated canonical output; raw comfort_ceiling_humidity stays internal, legacy comfort_humidity_display preserved disabled-by-default)
rows.notes: ROOMIQ-FRAMEWORK-001: customer Humidity Offset calibration applied once inside include/sense360/roomiq_engine.h; stale data goes unknown, never frozen.

rows.present_as: sensor s360_illuminance ("Illuminance", calibrated canonical output via the RoomIQ framework) over the compiled driver comfort_ceiling_illuminance (VEML7700 @0x10, internal)
rows.notes: An illuminance entity exists, but the live driver (VEML7700 @0x10) differs from the catalog BOM part (LTR-303ALS-01). ROOMIQ-FRAMEWORK-001 deliberately changed no compiled sensor, names the customer entity "Illuminance" (never a part name) and tracks the identity in the bench checklist; reconcile against the landed schematic/BOM before locking.

rows.present_as: binary_sensor ld2450_occupancy + target sensors (Radar Presence, Moving/Still Target, Target 1-3 X/Y/Speed/Distance/Angle)
binary_sensor s360_sen0609_presence (diagnostic, disabled by default) feeding the fused Occupancy / Presence Status entities
rows.notes: Filled by PRESENCE-FRAMEWORK-001 (executed ENTITY-FILL-200-SEN0609-001): digital presence output on roomiq_sen0609_output_pin (GPIO6); the roomiq_sen0609_uart bus (115200) stays Core-owned and reserved — ESPHome 2026.4.5 has no supported C4001/SEN0609 UART component. Compile/simulation proof only; no hardware verification claimed.

rows.present_as: binary_sensor s360_pir_motion (diagnostic, disabled by default) feeding the fused Occupancy / Presence Status entities
rows.notes: Filled by PRESENCE-FRAMEWORK-001 (executed ENTITY-FILL-200-PIR-001): GPIO binary_sensor on pir_sensor_pin (GPIO15, schematic PIR net). A bare GPIO level cannot prove sensor health (documented limitation). Compile/simulation proof only; no hardware verification claimed.
BOM lists BMP581 pressure on the RoomIQ +3.3V rail; no pressure entity exists in the RoomIQ YAML today.

rows.row: Comfort indices exposed as HA entities (dew point, heat index, comfort index)
rows.present_as: text_sensor s360_comfort ("Comfort") + s360_environment_state ("Environment State") + s360_brightness ("Brightness"); heat index via comfort_feels_like and the legacy 0-100 index via comfort_score_display (both disabled-by-default compatibility entities)
rows.notes: Resolved by the accepted ROOMIQ-FRAMEWORK-001 owner decisions (executed FEATURE-CONFIRM-200-COMFORT-INDICES-001): human-friendly comfort states instead of a customer-facing numeric score. Dew point remains an internal legacy global only (no owner ask to expose it); all thresholds are provisional comfort heuristics, compile/simulation proof only.

boards.notes: Comprehensive air-quality board. Catalog BOM: CO2 SCD41, VOC SGP41, gas MICS-4514 with STM8, connectors for SPS30 PM and SFA40 HCHO. The YAML exposes SCD41, SGP41, SPS30 and BMP390 fully; the MICS-4514 gas sensor and the SFA40 HCHO connector surface NO entity today.

rows.present_as: sensor airiq_co2 (+ SCD41 temperature/humidity)
sensor airiq_voc_index, airiq_nox_index
sensor airiq_pm1_0/pm2_5/pm4_0/pm10_0 + PMC counts + particle size + SPS30 clean button
rows.notes: Connector-attached sensor but live in the canonical ceiling base.

rows.present_as: sensor airiq_pressure (+ BMP390 temperature)
rows.notes: Live in YAML (ceiling only). The catalog BOM line for AirIQ does not call out a pressure part by name; confirm BMP390 against the landed BOM.
BOM lists MICS-4514 gas sensor with an STM8 co-processor; no gas entity exists today. Interface (STM8 readout path) must be confirmed from the schematic before filling.

BOM/pin-map lists an SFA40 HCHO connector; no HCHO entity exists. SFA40 is the newer Sensirion HCHO module (supersedes the SFA30 originally recorded here); its connector interface/address is verify-pending in the AirIQ pin-map (bus, I2C address, and ESPHome driver remain unconfirmed). The queued ENTITY-FILL-210-HCHO-001 fill slice now targets the SFA40 and still depends on interface confirmation.

boards.notes: Bathroom air-quality + ventilation-intent board. Catalog BOM: SGP41 on board, connectors for IR temp and SPS30. Base YAML adds SHT4x + BMP390 and derived bathroom logic (humidity rate, dew point, shower/mold/odor). The IR-temp (MLX90614) and PM (SPS30) connector sensors are not exposed in the canonical base.

rows.present_as: sensor bathroom_voc_index, bathroom_nox_index
sensor bathroom_temperature, bathroom_humidity
rows.notes: SHT4x is live in YAML but is not called out on the VentIQ catalog BOM line; confirm against the landed BOM.

rows.present_as: sensor bathroom_pressure (+ BMP390 temperature)
rows.notes: Live in YAML; not named on the VentIQ catalog BOM line. Confirm against the landed BOM.
BOM lists an IR-temp connector. The MLX90614 entities are not present in the canonical s360-211-ventiq.yaml base. Decide whether the base should carry it.
BOM lists an SPS30 connector. PM entities are not present in the canonical base.

rows.row: Bathroom logic features (shower active, mold risk, odor, condensation, fan recommendation)
rows.present_as: binary_sensor bathroom_shower_sensor, bathroom_mold_warning, bathroom_odor_sensor; global bathroom_fan_recommendation
rows.notes: Shower/mold/odor/condensation binary sensors exist; the fan-recommendation is a global, not an entity, and no fan-control surface is bound on VentIQ itself. Confirm the intended ventilation feature set (and whether bathroom_fan_recommendation should drive an HA control).

boards.notes: WS2812B addressable halo-ring indicator board (preview-gated for WebFlash). LED-FRAMEWORK-001: the board package defines the customer Room Light (hardware driver layer); the behaviour layers (Night Mode, Night Mode Behaviour, Status Indicator, Identify Device, Darkness Threshold, priority arbitration, restore contract) live in packages/features/led_framework.yaml, composed only by the LED-bearing bundles. LED-FRAMEWORK-002: RoomIQ (darkness) and Presence (occupancy) are OPTIONAL inputs (led_has_roomiq / led_has_presence, default false) read from the shared engine singletons; the full framework compiles with neither, and unsupported Night Mode Behaviour options fall back to Manual with a published capability notice.

rows.row: LED ring room light + approved effects (Gentle Pulse/Night Glow)
rows.present_as: light led_ring ("Room Light")
rows.notes: 12-LED WS2812B on GPIO38 (ceiling base). LED-FRAMEWORK-001 replaced the pre-framework effect showcase (Pulse/Alert/Random/Rainbow/Scan/Color Wipe) with the approved customer set; no strobe or novelty effects ship by default.

rows.present_as: light led_ring ("Room Light") native Home Assistant brightness control
rows.notes: LED-FRAMEWORK-001 replaced the pre-framework template number (led_brightness_control) with the Room Light's native brightness; the software maximum is the documented provisional led_max_brightness_pct substitution (no verified electrical/thermal ceiling exists - physical validation pending).

rows.present_as: switch s360_led_night_mode ("Night Mode")
rows.notes: LED-FRAMEWORK-001: engine-arbitrated Night Mode (provisional low/warm profile) with Manual / When dark / When dark and occupied behaviours; restores the previous customer state on turn-off. Replaced the pre-framework board-level switch (led_night_mode_switch).

rows.row: Dedicated HA night-light light entity (distinct from the status ring)
rows.notes: C3 WORKED EXAMPLE: whether a distinct night-light `light` entity (separate from the Room Light) is intended is product intent - operator must confirm before any fill. Do not fabricate it. LED-FRAMEWORK-001 deliberately implements Night Mode as a switch applying a profile to the single Room Light and leaves this gap open.

rows.row: Air-quality halo behaviour (LED colour mapped to AQI)
rows.present_as: feature packages ceiling_led_ring_air_quality.yaml / ceiling_halo_leds.yaml (layered, not bound by the board base)
rows.notes: Air-quality halo is a FEATURE layered on top of the LED hardware (needs an AirIQ source). The board base cross-references it but does not bind it. Confirm whether the LED product should ship the AQ halo by default.

boards.notes: On/off relay driver for bathroom fans. NO board-layer package exists (packages/boards/). Entities live only in the expansion packages/expansions/fan_relay.yaml, which proxies the Core main_relay. Board package + product surface are owned by the PACKAGE-RELAY-001 / PRODUCT-RELAY-001 chain (see config/kit-intent-matrix.json and docs/hardware/package-readiness-matrix.md).

rows.present_as: switch fan_relay_switch (proxies Core main_relay)
rows.notes: A relay switch exists in the expansion layer, but there is no canonical S360-310 board package. Schematic is cataloged_unverified; board-layer ownership is blocked on PACKAGE-RELAY-001.

rows.row: Fan on/off + speed/preset HA control surface
rows.present_as: fan_control_profile.yaml exists but is not bound to a relay board
rows.notes: A relay is on/off, not variable-speed; confirm the intended HA fan UX (simple switch vs fan entity) for the relay product.

boards.notes: 12V PWM fan driver, up to 4 fans with tach feedback. NO board-layer package. Driver lives in packages/expansions/fan_pwm_native.yaml (native ESP32-S3 GPIO; legacy SX1509 variants superseded). Compile/validation pending per the PWM lane.

rows.row: PWM fan drive outputs + fan entities (up to 4)
rows.present_as: output fan_pwm_native_drive_1..4 (ledc) + fan fan_pwm_native_1..4
rows.notes: Fan outputs/entities exist in the expansion but compile validation is pending and there is no canonical board package. Pin map is partial; schematic cataloged_unverified.

rows.present_as: sensor fan_pwm_native_tach_1/2/4 (pulse_counter)
rows.notes: Tach 1/2/4 exist but RPM is unvalidated; Pul_Cou3 (GPIO46) is disabled (collision with fan_status_led_pin) and TachIO (GPIO16) role is ambiguous. Reconcile against the landed schematic.

rows.row: Fan speed / preset HA control surface
rows.present_as: fan_control_profile.yaml / fan_control_advanced_profile.yaml (quiet mode, auto mode, RPM display, link mode)
rows.notes: Basic/advanced fan-control profiles exist but are not bound to a PWM board product. Confirm which preset/speed/quiet/auto features the PWM product should expose.

boards.notes: 0-10V analog fan driver (GP8403 I2C DAC). NO board-layer package. Driver lives in packages/expansions/fan_gp8403.yaml as neutral package-layer outputs only (no user-facing fan entity in the driver).

rows.present_as: gp8403 fan_dac_1/2 + output fan_dac_1/2_vout0/1 (package-layer, no user entity)
rows.notes: DAC outputs exist at the package layer with no user-facing fan/voltage entity, and no canonical board package. Schematic cataloged_unverified.

rows.row: Fan speed / voltage HA control surface
rows.present_as: fan_control_advanced_profile.yaml exists (dual-channel, voltage display) but is not bound
rows.notes: Confirm the intended 0-10V fan UX (fan entity, output voltage display, dual-channel link) for the DAC product. webflash-compatibility grammar forbids FanDAC+AirIQ, so the duct-fan context applies.

boards.notes: Phase-dimmer for mains fan or lamp. NO board-layer package. Driver lives in packages/expansions/fan_triac.yaml and stays BLOCKED from exposure: publish is gated by PACKAGE-TRIAC-001 (signed attestation) + the COMPLIANCE-001-RESOLUTION-001 experimental-lane preconditions (COMPLIANCE-001 closed by market posture — S360-320 is never placed on the market; see docs/decisions/COMPLIANCE-001-RESOLUTION-001.md). Historical note: the placeholder-pin / SX1509 buildability blocker (HW-005) was resolved by TRIAC-PINMAP-CORRECT-001.

rows.row: AC phase-cut dimmer output + fan entity
rows.present_as: output fan_triac_output (ac_dimmer) + fan fan_controller + sensor fan_speed_percent
rows.notes: Entities exist in the expansion but pins are unresolved/BLOCKED (HW-005, HW-PINMAP-320-FOLLOWUP) and there is no board package. Mains-voltage compliance is open.

rows.row: Fan speed / preset HA control surface
rows.notes: Confirm intended TRIAC fan UX; product stays advanced/manual or blocked until HW + compliance clear.

boards.notes: Mains-to-5V PSU (HLK-5M05). NO board-layer package. Diagnostic-only entities live in packages/hardware/power_240v.yaml. Compliance-blocked (pwr_is_compliance_blocked per config/kit-intent-matrix.json).

rows.row: Power diagnostics (supply voltage, power source, AC connected)
rows.present_as: sensor supply_voltage + text_sensor power_source_indicator/power_config_info + binary_sensor ac_power_connected
rows.notes: Diagnostic entities exist in the hardware power package but there is no canonical board package and the schematic is cataloged_unverified. Compliance-blocked.

boards.notes: PoE-to-5V supply (IEEE 802.3af). Diagnostic-only board package; no GPIO/I2C/UART hardware control.

rows.row: Power diagnostics (supply voltage, power source, PoE connected)
rows.present_as: sensor supply_voltage + text_sensor power_source_indicator/power_config_info + binary_sensor poe_power_connected
rows.notes: Diagnostic entities present. Schematic cataloged_unverified (HW-PINMAP-410 pending); values are fixed/synthetic, not measured.
